sources.label: Investing.com - Rogers Corp stock hits 52-week high at $144.59 (May 26, 2026; notes 107% 1-yr run, InvestingPro overvalued flag, Q1 EPS $0.75 in line / sales slight miss, El-Haj permanent CEO)
StockTitan - Rogers 8-K: shareholders back directors, pay, 2026 ESPP (annual meeting May 6, 2026)
Yahoo Finance - ROG quote and latest headlines (no fresh material items June 6-9)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already moved ROG up on this page and nothing new has emerged since June 6; today's -1.15% is drift, not a setup change. The late-May 52-week high after a 107% one-year run (with no fresh design wins or guidance) keeps eroding residual alpha at the margin, hence a small negative drift rather than another rank move.
verdicts.bull: Sub-$2.5B underfollowed materials name with real thermal/laminate exposure to AI power and high-speed signal chains; new permanent CEO executing on margins (gross margin 32.2% vs 29.9% in Q1).
verdicts.neutral: No news since June 6; stock consolidating ~4% below its $144.59 high. Q1 was in line on EPS but light on sales, so the AI-leverage thesis is still more narrative than printed revenue.
verdicts.bear: Up 51% YTD and ~107% in a year on multiple expansion, flagged as overvalued versus fair value; if Q2 (July 30) shows the same flat ~$200M sales run-rate, the rerating unwinds and residual alpha here was already spent.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 earnings on July 30 showing AI/power-electronics demand converting to revenue acceleration
verdicts.primaryRisk: Post-rerating crowding: a second consecutive sales miss compresses the multiple on a stock that has already doubled in a year

verdicts.rankRationale: On the network-centrality lens nothing changed: Rogers' position in thermal-interface, high-frequency laminates and power-electronics substrates is intact, and there is no new contract, capacity or regulatory node-level information since June 6. Hold at rank 7 per yesterday's verdict.
verdicts.bull: Curamik/thermal and high-frequency materials sit at a genuine physical chokepoint for AI accelerators, power conversion and RF; margin recovery under the new CEO confirms operational leverage into any electronics upcycle.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis is centrality-based, not price-based, so the -1.15% day and the post-high consolidation are noise; node importance is unchanged pending Q2 evidence of demand pull-through.
verdicts.bear: Centrality has limits: Rogers' end-mix is still heavily EV/industrial/defense, so an AI-driven rerating could outrun the actual AI revenue node, and activist/cyclical sensitivity can swamp the structural story.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Concrete AI/data-center design-win or capacity disclosures (thermal-interface, power substrate) at or before Q2 earnings
verdicts.primaryRisk: EV and industrial cyclicality masking or reversing the AI-adjacent demand signal in reported numbers
